site.tagline: Inspiring Growth. Empowering Success. Leading with Empathy.
site.subtitle: Inspiring Growth. Empowering Success. Leading with Empathy.
site.footerNote: Built with Heart, Purpose, and Passion

introStatement: I am committed to helping others achieve their goals and dedicated to creating positive change. Professionally, my focus is on expanding access to essential resources for individuals with disabilities. Working as a home care aid with quadriplegic patients for many years inspired my passion for advocating for equitable access to essential resources regardless of income, to ensure that individuals receive the support they need. To me, leadership means creating an inclusive environment where all team members feel heard and valued. My priorities are my family, education, spirituality, and inspiring equity and opportunity in all aspects of my life, both professionally and personally.

education.degree: Bachelor of Applied Science, BAS-OLTM - Organizational Leadership & Technical Management
education.description: Focused on leadership, strategy, collaboration, and inclusive organizational growth.

education.degree: Associate of Applied Science in Medical Administration
education.description: Built strong administrative, communication, and healthcare systems knowledge.

workExperience.description: Serve as a peer mentor to 200+ college students, helping Scholars to navigate academic and professional resources, supporting them in college and WSOS processes, sharing opportunities, and building a sense of community.
Support quadriplegic patients with daily living activities, physical care, emotional well-being, and greater independence in the home environment.
Oversaw day-to-day operations, led a team of approximately 15 employees, managed hiring and training, and improved workflow, service quality, and inventory control.

skills: Ability to Multitask and Manage Multiple Projects

projects.title: COIL Project: Final Group Presentation on COIL & Social Media Influences
projects.description:
The Collaborative Online International Learning (COIL) Project was a five-week virtual collaboration between students from Lower Columbia College (LCC) and the University of Sharjah in the United Arab Emirates. This project focused on exploring cultural differences in digital identity and social media use, allowing us to compare perspectives shaped by distinct cultural and societal norms. Through cross-cultural collaboration, I developed stronger communication skills, particularly in navigating time zones, language nuances, and differing viewpoints. I also strengthened my leadership and teamwork skills by contributing to group discussions and coordinating our final presentation.

This project is especially relevant to my academic and professional development because it enhanced my cultural competence, which is an essential skill in today's globalized and diverse workplace. Understanding how culture influences communication and digital behavior prepares me to work more effectively in team environments, particularly in roles that require collaboration across diverse populations. This experience reinforced the importance of adaptability, empathy, and inclusive communication.

accomplishments: Mentor and support over 200 college scholars through WSOS.
Led teams and operations in both nonprofit and business environments.
Advanced academic goals while balancing leadership, service, family, and professional responsibilities.
Advocated for equitable access to essential resources and inclusive support systems.

volunteerWork.description: Served as treasurer in Longview, Washington, helping support youth athletics and community engagement.

Supported service projects, student leadership, and community outreach efforts.

Support scholars in navigating college and WSOS processes, collaborate with staff and leads to find resources, assist scholars in connecting with academic and community support, provide quarterly opportunities, and help create a strong scholar community.
Volunteer judge supporting student achievement and leadership development.
Contributed volunteer support for athletic and community events.

certifications: Microsoft Office Suite (Word, Excel, PowerPoint, OneNote, Teams, & Outlook)
